subject: Anthropology
title: Fair Trade
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Fair Trade.

hook.text: The fair trade label promises ethical coffee. But does the farmer actually benefit, or does it ease consumer guilt?

buildup.text: Fair trade aims to guarantee better prices, decent conditions, and sustainability for producers in developing nations.

discovery.text: It challenges pure market logic by adding ethical criteria to exchange — fairness becomes part of the price.

twist.text: Studies show mixed results. Farmers often receive only slightly more. Certification costs can exclude the poorest.

climax.text: Fair trade shows that markets can be moralized. But systemic change needs more than labels — it needs structural reform.

punchline.text: Ethical buying is a start, not a solution.

quiz.question: What is the goal of fair trade?
options: Guaranteeing better prices and conditions for developing-world producers
